description_long: This is a roughly rectangular, pale stone slab featuring a prominent fossilized fish embedded on one of its broad faces. The stone has an uneven, natural surface with visible textural variations and subtle color gradations from off-white to light beige. The fossil itself is well-defined, with the outline and internal structure of the fish clearly visible in brownish hues, contrasting with the lighter stone matrix. The edges of the slab are irregular and appear to have been broken or chipped, giving it an authentic, unrefined appearance. The reverse side of the stone is plain and unmarked, while the narrow sides reveal the thickness and layered composition of the material. This object would likely serve as a decorative or educational feature in an urban park, museum, or landscaped public space.
description: A pale, irregular stone slab with a detailed fossilized fish embedded on one face, featuring natural textures and color variations.
description_view_0: The broad face displays the fossilized fish in detail, with visible textures and color contrasts.
description_view_1: A narrow side view showing the slab's thickness and slightly uneven edges.
description_view_2: The reverse side is plain, smooth, and unmarked, highlighting the stone's natural coloration.
description_view_3: Another narrow side view, similar to view 1, emphasizing the slab's depth and rough edge.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature on a display plinth or embedded in landscaping
attribute_pattern: Distinct fossilized fish pattern on one face, with irregular natural stone textures and subtle color mottling.
attribute_weathering: Slightly rough and aged, with minor chipping and surface irregularities indicating some exposure to elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with manicured urban landscaping or modern structures, but can blend with naturalistic rock features or educational displays.
